essence: Designs and improves the machines, systems, and mechanisms that keep the physical world moving.

identity.title: Mechanical Engineer
identity.summary: Designs, analyses, and tests mechanical systems and components, applying physics and engineering principles to solve practical problems.

salary.notes: Specialist sectors such as aerospace, energy, and automotive often pay higher. Chartered status increases earning potential.

qualifications: Strong Understanding of Physics and Materials
Experience with CAD and Simulation Tools

lifestyle.description: Mechanical engineering blends analytical thinking with hands‑on problem‑solving. Days often involve designing components, running simulations, testing prototypes, and collaborating with manufacturing teams. The work can be highly technical, requiring precision and patience, especially when troubleshooting failures or refining designs under tight constraints. Projects may span months or even years, offering a deep sense of ownership and craftsmanship. The lifestyle suits individuals who enjoy physics, practical engineering, and the satisfaction of seeing ideas become real, functional systems.
